title: fix: h100-mfu-calculation
body: I was getting 104% MFU on h100 then i realized that MFU calculation might of been based on a100 312 tflops h100 is 989 tflops at bfloat16. nvidia claims 1,979tflops but that is for 2:4 sparsity.

step_plan.summary: Fixes MFU reporting on H100 by removing the implicit A100-based normalization in the MFU calculation and using an H100-appropriate (or user-configurable) BF16 peak FLOPs denominator so MFU no longer exceeds 100% under normal conditions.

steps.operation: Make MFU normalization device-aware and/or configurable by replacing the hard-coded A100 peak FLOPs constant with a selectable peak BF16 TFLOPs (e.g., A100=312e12, H100=989e12) and updating the docstring/comments accordingly
steps.reason: The current MFU computation is normalized by an A100 BF16 peak (312 TFLOPs), which can yield MFU > 100% on H100; selecting the correct denominator (or allowing override) fixes the reported utilization while preserving prior A100 behavior.
steps.side_effects: MFU values in logs/wandb will change on non-A100 GPUs (notably H100). If a lookup is added using torch.cuda device name/compute capability, behavior on unknown GPUs must be defined (fallback to A100 or require explicit override). Any signature change to estimate_mfu would require updates to its callers (e.g., train.py).
